entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A tropical crop, specifically Pennisetum typhoides and P. americanum, investigated for temperature responses.

entities.name: LEAF AREA DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The growth and expansion of leaf area in pearl millet cultivars.

entities.name: LIGHT INTERCEPTION
entities.description: The process of capturing light by the crop canopy.

entities.name: DRY MATTER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The accumulation of biomass, referred to as DM production.

entities.name: MEAN AIR TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The environmental temperature variable tested at 19.5, 21.0, 26.0, and 31.0°C.

entities.name: LIGHT TRANSMISSION COEFFICIENT
entities.description: A measure of light passing through the canopy, varying between cultivars.

entities.name: EFFICIENCY OF ENERGY CONVERSION
entities.description: The conversion of light energy into biomass, measured in g/MJ.

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The initial stage of seed growth.

entities.name: EARLY ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The phase of growth following germination.

entities.name: SANIO
entities.description: A specific cultivar of pearl millet with a light transmission coefficient of 0.47.

entities.name: OASIS
entities.description: A specific cultivar of pearl millet with a light transmission coefficient of 0.23.

relationships.description: Influences the capacity for light capture
Influences the rate of biomass accumulation
Exhibits variation in leaf area development
Exhibits variation in dry matter production
Exhibits variation in light transmission coefficient
Exhibits variation in energy conversion efficiency
Exhibits genotypic differences in early establishment
